title: MLS join_from_welcome_for_group does not validate that the Welcome's embedded group_id matches the caller-supplied group_id
description: core/src/mls.rs join_from_welcome_for_group: takes a group_id parameter which is used as the key in self.groups, but the Welcome message itself encodes the group_id that was used to create the group. If these diverge (e.g. a caller supplies the wrong group_id to the map key), the group is stored under the wrong key — subsequent encrypt_for_group and process_for_group calls with the canonical group_id will not find it. The openmls StagedWelcome → into_group() does validate the Welcome cryptographically but doesn't expose the embedded group_id for comparison. At minimum, a comment explaining this invariant is required. A contributor adding a new group type could easily supply the wrong group_id.
